main.title: Why Are There No More Einsteins?
main.description:
Try to name a living genius. You can't, and neither can we. What actually happened to the Einsteins is a lot weirder than you think.

What you'll learn:
- The absurd thing one bored patent clerk pulled off in a single year
- Why your phone's GPS secretly depends on it every day
- The crack in the "lone genius" story almost everyone still believes
- Where the geniuses of today actually went (they're not gone)

Could you name a single living genius? Try it in the comments.

SOURCES
- Einstein's 1905 "annus mirabilis": patent clerk, age 26, thesis rejected; photoelectric effect (his Nobel), Brownian motion, special relativity, E=mc². (Britannica; arXiv.)
- GPS requires relativistic correction; ~2 km/day drift without it. (Institute for Advanced Study.)
- The "lone genius" is partly mythologized. (Big Think; Caltech "Not a Lone Genius.")
- The "burden of knowledge": breakthroughs need ever-larger teams. (Bloom, Jones, Van Reenen & Webb 2020.)
- Genius = nature AND conditions. (IQ heritability literature; Flynn effect.)

This video uses AI-assisted narration and illustrations.

#einstein #genius #science #history #wonder
tags: why are there no more einsteins
